category: MA=1 cm, MB=3 cm, AB=2 cm implies collinear with A between M and B; net field is about 3.2e5 N/C, not 4.0e4 N/C.
question: Two charges q₁ = +4 × 10^-9 C and q₂ = –4 × 10^-9 C are placed at points A and B, separated by 2 cm. Calculate the magnitude of the electric field strength at point M such that MA = 1 cm and MB = 3 cm.
reason: MA=1 cm, MB=3 cm, AB=2 cm implies collinear with A between M and B; net field is about 3.2e5 N/C, not 4.0e4 N/C.

category: Coulomb force vector sum using CA=10 cm, CB=8 cm, AB=9 cm gives about 1.48e-3 N; expected 1.480 N is off by 1000x.
question: Two point charges q1 = -4 × 10^-8 C and q2 = -9 × 10^-8 C are placed at points A and B, which are 9 cm apart in the air. A third charge q3 = -1 × 10^-8 C is placed at point C, such that the distance from C to A is 10 cm and the distance from C to B is 8 cm. Calculate the net electric force acting on q3.
reason: Coulomb force vector sum using CA=10 cm, CB=8 cm, AB=9 cm gives about 1.48e-3 N; expected 1.480 N is off by 1000x.

category: Three equal charges 5e-6 C on equilateral triangle side 10 cm give sqrt(3) k q^2/a^2 ≈ 38.9 N; expected 3.89e-3 N is off by 1e4.
question: Three electric charges, q1 = q2 = q3 = +5 × 10^-6 C, are placed at the three vertices of an equilateral triangle of side length 10 cm in the air. Calculate the resultant electric force acting on q3.
reason: Three equal charges 5e-6 C on equilateral triangle side 10 cm give sqrt(3) k q^2/a^2 ≈ 38.9 N; expected 3.89e-3 N is off by 1e4.

category: Symmetric dipole geometry with q_test=1e-8 C gives net force about 0.230 N; expected 0.230e-3 N is off by 1000x.
question: Two point charges, q1 = +8 × 10^-6 C and q2 = -8 × 10^-6 C, are placed at points A and B, separated by 12 cm. A test charge q = 10^-8 C is placed at point M, which lies on the perpendicular bisector of AB, 4 cm away from AB. Calculate the net electric force acting on q. Give your answer rounded to three decimal places.
reason: Symmetric dipole geometry with q_test=1e-8 C gives net force about 0.230 N; expected 0.230e-3 N is off by 1000x.

category: At the right-angle vertex of an isosceles right triangle, equal perpendicular fields combine by sqrt(2), giving about 5.64e6 V/m; expected 7.95e6 equals scalar 2E, not vector sum.
question: Three identical charges, q = 1.2 × 10^-6 C, are placed at the three vertices of an isosceles right triangle with legs of length 5.2 cm. Calculate the net electric field at the right-angle vertex. Give your answer two decimal places.
reason: At the right-angle vertex of an isosceles right triangle, equal perpendicular fields combine by sqrt(2), giving about 5.64e6 V/m; expected 7.95e6 equals scalar 2E, not vector sum.
